rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Line 11. Defendant George F. Schuler, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 438(e). While that section does state that a late motion for judgment on the pleadings may be brought if the court permits, the court never granted an application for leave to file an untimely motion. (See Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1063.) Plaintiff¿s objection based on timeliness is sustained. Even if the court did consider the motion, it would be denied because grounds do not appear from the four corners of the complaint. Defendant¿s request for judicial notice of the order granting defendant Robertshaw Controls¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentativeand must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Defendant George F. Schuler, Inc.S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Line 11. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall Inc.¿s joinder in George F. Schuler, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 438(e). While that section does state that a late motion for judgment on the pleadings may be brought if the court permits, the court never granted an application for leave to file an untimely motion. (See Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1063.) Even if the court did consider the motion, it would be denied because grounds do not appear from the four corners of the complaint. Moving defendant¿s request for judicial notice of the order granting defendant Robertshaw Controls¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant¿s proof of service shows that it personally served its joinder on July 27, making its joinder timely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order repeating verbatim of the tentative to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance w/ CRC 3.1312.= (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant George F. Schuler, Inc.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Line 11. Defendants Swinerton Builders and Perini Corp.¿s joinder in George F. Schuler, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as untimely. Defendants failed to give proper notice required by C.C.P. sections 1005(b) and 1010.6. The last day to provide proper notice by e-service was August 26 and defendants e-served their joinder on July 30. Plaintiffs¿ objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY ROBERTSON et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PERNIZ VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiff¿s first amended complaint is denied as to punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Plaintiff¿s complaint (Brayton Purcell Master Complaint and Summary Complaint) alleges facts sufficient to plead a claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff dismissed his false representation cause of action. Plaintiff¿s objection that defendant¿s notice failed to state the grounds upon which it sought relief is overruled. Although the notice failed to state any grounds the supporting papers make clear the grounds for the relief sought. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order repeating verbatim of the tentative to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
